mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals strong real economic expansion with stable pricing, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL reverses, or 60-day forward returns show negative exposure to the combined signal.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles characterized by FEDFUNDS above 4.5% compress bond yields and increase equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 115 signals robust manufacturing activity that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 115.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1150 thousand units signals weak demand for construction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST falls below 1150.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signals strong labor market and demand conditions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The co-movement between PAYEMS and RSAFS breaks down, or 60-day forward returns show negative exposure to the combined signal.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) above 3% signals strong economic growth that drives equity valuations higher, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 exceeds 3% growth.
